note: Two canon corrections (Erik 2026). (1) The axes are CULTURAL/ENERGETIC disposition, not literal geography — a quality that saturates a people and their land, discovered by moving among peoples, not by walking miles on a grid. (2) 'The Valley' is ONE area, the first detailed; the content pack named 'valley/' is really the WORLD pack. This doc is the authoritative framing; the folder name is legacy and can be renamed later without breaking the manifest. Authored by Aevi (PO).

axes_are_disposition.principle: A Reach or a pole-intensity is not a place on a map you could survey. It is a CHARACTER OF PLACE-AND-PEOPLE — an ambient energy and a cultural temperature. A people can be 'order-steeped' the way a culture is formal; a valley can 'feel' grieving or teeming. The pole-intensities live in inhabitants, customs, mood, and the felt energy of the land, not in coordinates.
why_it_matters: Co-firing makes sense as disposition: a people can be death-steeped AND teeming with life at once (the Quickwood) because it's a quality, not a location — two energies in one culture.
The map is a map of CHARACTERS-OF-PLACE, discovered by encountering peoples and their felt energy, not by measuring distance. Adjacency = cultural/energetic kinship, not miles.

A traveler doesn't 'walk up the order-gradient' geographically so much as move deeper into order-steeped peoples and lands until the energy is overwhelming. The pole is where a disposition runs pure enough to be dangerous.
Individuals carry pole-intensities too (see innate_talents, and a character's own drift): a person IS a small coordinate of dispositions. The land and its people share and shape each other's energy.
axes_are_disposition.engine_note: poleIntensity on a location = the ambient cultural/energetic disposition of that place-and-people. The Rune Dice read this disposition; affinities flex from it; drift moves a character's own disposition toward the poles they steep in. Nothing changes mechanically — only the FRAMING: describe places by their energy and people, not their grid position. The GM narrates a Reach as a felt quality, never as a region on a literal map.

this_is_a_world.principle: The Valley of Echoes is ONE area — the first detailed, the starting basin. It is not the world. The content pack currently named 'valley/' holds world-wide content (the Reaches, the legends, the coordinate model, the far poles) and is properly the WORLD pack.
corrections: Wherever lore says 'the valley' as if it were everything, read 'the known world' or the specific area meant. The Valley of Echoes is a named basin; the Reaches and poles are elsewhere in the world.

The four original power systems (Harmonic, Radiant, Valley Craft, Precursor) are the crafts of the VALLEY basin specifically — its local folk-traditions. The Reach traditions (Vivimancy, the Edge, Soma, etc.) are the crafts of OTHER parts of the world. This is already true in structure; the framing just makes it explicit.
Character origins should eventually be able to start OUTSIDE the Valley (a Marcher of the Redline, a Churnfolk maker, a Rootkin of the Quickwood) — not only Valley-natives. See creation_update spec.
'Begin in Millbrook' (the hardcoded start) is the VALLEY's starting town — correct for a Valley origin, wrong as a universal default. Start location should follow origin.
this_is_a_world.rename_note: Do NOT rename the valley/ folder mid-flight (breaks manifest + CCode in-flight). Flagged for a later clean rename to world/ (or a world/ pack alongside a valley/ area-pack). Canon is corrected here now; the folder catches up later.

bearers_vary.principle: A pole is borne by whatever can hold it — NOT always a full civilization. Bearer-form tracks how PURE and how LIVABLE the disposition is: a CIVILIZATION occupies the livable near-pole (functioning at scale); a RELIGION or ORDER organizes around the axis as devotion; a CULT OF PURITY occupies the extreme, where the axis runs pure. Refinement (Erik 2026-07-11) atop the reach-civilizations model.
why_it_matters: A pure pole is UNLIVABLE at civilization-scale — no people can sustain absolute falsehood, death, or order and still function as a people. Only a cult can hold the pure extreme, because a cult does not have to WORK, only to BELIEVE. Civilizations cluster NEAR their pole; the true extremes are held by orders and cults of purity.

This maps the framework's evil-as-contraction onto geography: the cult of the pure pole IS the foreclosure — a people who stopped propagating and only intensified. The reach-civilizations (Rootkin, Marchers, Umbrals, Blazeborn) are the livable near-pole; past them, at the true extreme, live the cults that worship the pole alone and have burned everything else away.

Bearer-type is readable from position: near-pole = civilization; deeper = order/religion; the pure LOCUS (already canon — the Redline, the Blaze, the Churn) is where the cult, not the civilization, lives.

cultures_are_profiles.principle: A culture is not a single axis-pole — it is a multi-axis DISPOSITION PROFILE: a primary pole plus secondary leans, a full spectrum fingerprint like a location or a character. The Radiants/Blazeborn are LIGHT-primary but also ORDER-leaning (revelation tends toward structure). Refinement (Erik 2026-07-11).
why_it_matters: This is why adjacency=kinship reads as a smooth field, not single-axis pins: neighboring peoples share SECONDARY leans. The Valley crossing is order+light+practical because the peoples there — order-leaning Harmonics, light-and-order Radiants, practical valleyfolk — share those secondary axes; that shared profile is WHY they are neighbors.
A generated or authored people carries a spectrum fingerprint (primary + 1-2 secondary leans), not a single tag. Its crafts, customs, and neighbors follow from the whole profile.

cultures_are_distributions.principle: A culture is a DISTRIBUTION, not a monolith — the spread of lives its disposition tends to produce, with a center of gravity and heterodox tails. Among the light-and-order Radiants you find the rare light-and-CHAOS practitioner; the heretic at the edge is not a bug but the culture's interiority. Refinement (Erik 2026-07-11); extends 'individuals carry pole-intensities' — the clone-tree/variance principle at civilization scale.
why_it_matters: A people is a center others scatter around. A generated member should sometimes come back OFF the cultural norm on purpose — that variance is what gives a culture texture, internal conflict, and reasons for story.
Orthodoxy vs heresy is a live axis within every people: the pure-pole cult is orthodoxy taken to foreclosure; the heterodox tail is where a people stays alive to its opposite.

open_reconciliation_harmonic: CONFLICT to resolve (Erik): the_twelve_reaches places Harmonic Heights as 'leaning mechanical' (mechanical_spiritual axis), but Erik 2026-07-11 frames Harmonic as ORDER (chaos_order), next to Radiant in an order+light+practical crossing. The cultures_are_profiles model can hold both (Harmonic = order-primary + mechanical + sound-medium, OR mechanical-primary + order-secondary) — but which is PRIMARY is Erik's call. Flagged, not resolved.
